title: Sell or Process Further
event: Reyes Co. can sell a product at split-off for $20 per unit, or process it further at an additional cost of $5 per unit and sell it for $28. Joint costs of $6 per unit have already been incurred. Compute the incremental effect of processing further.

computationPaths.narration: Joint costs are sunk — spent identically under both choices. The decision lives entirely in the incremental revenue vs incremental cost.

memorize.body: Process further if incremental revenue > incremental processing cost. Joint (sunk) costs are ignored.
Subtracting the $6 joint cost from the analysis is the planted error — it was spent before the decision existed.

questions.prompt: Split-off price $20, processed price $28, extra processing $5, joint cost $6/unit. Incremental income of processing = ?

distractors.feedback: You charged the sunk $6 joint cost against the decision (8 − 5 − 6). It's already spent either way: +3.
That's incremental REVENUE alone. The $5 of extra processing cost comes out: +3.
